page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a virtual server instance configured with 6 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.67 GB per core. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-hypervised instance features 6 physical cores with single-threaded execution per core. The processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 64 KB of L1d cache. It supports hardware-level instructions such as AVX, AES cryptography, SHA_NI, and BMI2. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between single-core compute limits and memory performance. The instance registers poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%, and weak multi-core performance in the bottom 25%. However, memory bandwidth remains competitive, achieving average tier results with 616.67 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 43.56 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though large-block write bandwidth is weaker at 24.88 GB/sec. Geekbench metrics align with an average tier, showing stable performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 8.81 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is weaker at 5.86 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and moderate memory capacity, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute cores. It is well-suited for general-purpose tasks, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations. It can also handle small-scale LLM inference, processing 135M parameter models at average speeds, though performance drops significantly on larger 7B models. It is less suitable for high-throughput web serving or intensive multi-core processing due to its shared CPU architecture and lower multi-core benchmark ratings.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 6 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 16 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. This instance does not bundle local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: while single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests rank in the lower percentiles, memory read bandwidth and Geekbench scores for software compilation and image processing perform at average tier levels. Redis SET operations reach an average of 8.81 million operations per second. This resource profile provides a cost-effective solution for workloads with variable CPU demands, such as software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database tasks, while being less suited for sustained high-performance computing or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced memory bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server balancing six AMD EPYC vCPUs and sixteen gigabytes of memory for variable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 6 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (2.67 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Average-tier memory read bandwidth up to 616.67 GB/sec
Suitable for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight databases
